examples.title: Agile Knowledge Transfer Simulation
examples.description: Simulates knowledge sharing between team members through pair programming sessions
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SimulateKnowledgeTransfer(teamMembers, sessions)
 // Initialize knowledge matrix
 FOR each member i in teamMembers:
 FOR each member j in teamMembers:
 IF i == j THEN knowledge[i][j] = 1
 ELSE knowledge[i][j] = 0

 // Simulate pairing sessions
 FOR s = 1 to sessions:
 pairs = GenerateRandomPairs(teamMembers)
 FOR each pair (a, b) in pairs:
 // Bidirectional knowledge transfer
 knowledge[a][b] = 1
 knowledge[b][a] = 1
 // Transitive knowledge: a learns what b knows
 FOR each member c in teamMembers:
 IF knowledge[b][c] == 1 AND a != c:
 knowledge[a][c] = 1

 // Calculate team knowledge coverage
 coverage = Sum(knowledge) / (n * n)
 RETURN coverage
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This simulation demonstrates how pair programming sessions gradually distribute knowledge across a team. Initially, members only know their own expertise. After each pairing, bidirectional transfer occurs. Additionally, transitive knowledge transfer happens: if A pairs with B who knows C's expertise, A indirectly learns about C. The coverage metric measures overall team knowledge distribution.
examples.timeComplexity: O(s × p × n²) where s=sessions, p=pairs per session, n=team size

examples.title: Agile Sprint Knowledge Validation
examples.description: Algorithm for validating sprint backlog items against accumulated knowledge
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ValidateSprintKnowledge(backlogItems, teamKnowledge)
 validatedItems = []
 knowledgeGaps = []

 FOR each item in backlogItems:
 requiredSkills = item.GetRequiredSkills()
 requiredDomain = item.GetRequiredDomain()

 // Check if team has sufficient knowledge
 teamHasSkills = CheckSkillCoverage(teamKnowledge, requiredSkills)
 teamHasDomain = CheckDomainKnowledge(teamKnowledge, requiredDomain)

 IF teamHasSkills AND teamHasDomain THEN
 validatedItems.Add(item)
 ELSE
 // Identify knowledge gaps
 missingSkills = GetMissingSkills(requiredSkills, teamKnowledge)
 missingDomain = GetMissingDomain(requiredDomain, teamKnowledge)
 knowledgeGaps.Add(CreateGapItem(item, missingSkills, missingDomain))

 RETURN (validatedItems, knowledgeGaps)
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE CheckSkillCoverage(knowledge, requiredSkills)
 FOR each skill in requiredSkills:
 teamMembersWithSkill = 0
 FOR each member in knowledge:
 IF member.HasSkill(skill) THEN
 teamMembersWithSkill = teamMembersWithSkill + 1
 IF teamMembersWithSkill < MIN_SKILL_COVERAGE THEN
 RETURN FALSE
 RETURN TRUE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm validates whether the team's accumulated knowledge is sufficient for sprint backlog items. It checks both technical skills and domain knowledge coverage. If knowledge gaps exist, they are identified for remediation (through training, hiring, or external expertise). This prevents starting work the team cannot complete due to knowledge limitations.
examples.timeComplexity: O(b × s × m) where b=backlog items, s=skills per item, m=team members
examples.spaceComplexity: O(b + g) where g=knowledge gaps identified

examples.title: Retrospective Knowledge Capture System
examples.description: System for capturing and categorizing lessons learned during sprint retrospectives
examples.code:
DATA STRUCTURE LessonLearned:
 description: String
 category: Enum(Process, Technical, Communication, Domain)
 impact: Enum(Positive, Negative)
 actionItems: List<String>
 applicableTo: List[String] // project names or types

PROCEDURE CaptureRetrospective(lessons, sprintData)
 categorized = InitializeCategoryMap()

 FOR each lesson in lessons:
 // Classify the lesson
 category = ClassifyLesson(lesson, sprintData)
 impact = DetermineImpact(lesson)

 // Create structured lesson record
 record = CreateLessonRecord(
 description = lesson,
 category = category,
 impact = impact,
 actionItems = ExtractActions(lesson),
 applicableTo = DetermineScope(sprintData)
 )

 // Add to organizational knowledge base
 categorized[category].Add(record)

 // Persist for future reference
 StoreInKnowledgeBase(categorized)

 // Notify relevant teams
 FOR each category in categorized:
 relevantTeams = FindTeamsNeeding(category)
 Notify(relevantTeams, categorized[category])

 RETURN categorized
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This system structures lessons from retrospectives into reusable organizational knowledge. Each lesson is categorized (process, technical, communication, domain), its impact assessed, and actionable items extracted. The knowledge base can be queried by future teams facing similar situations. This institutionalizes learning from one sprint to benefit all future work.
examples.timeComplexity: O(l × c) where l=lessons, c=classification complexity
